title: Check Visible Fault Indicators During Startup Or Shutdown State Transitions
summary: Use the training source's examples to inspect whether a startup or shutdown transition is delayed or incomplete because of a visible system fault or a visible AGV waiting condition.
when_to_use: Use when the system appears to be in startup or shutdown and the transition seems delayed, incomplete, or not progressing as expected, and you need to determine whether the source-described visible fault or waiting examples are present.
not_for: Do not use as a recovery procedure to clear faults or force completion of startup or shutdown.
Do not use when corrective action requires steps not described in this source.
Do not use as a complete troubleshooting workflow beyond observing visible faults or waiting conditions during startup or shutdown.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Observe startup or shutdown behavior, check for visible system fault indications, compare observed conditions to the source-described examples, and document what is visible.

safety_notes: This source provides diagnostic observation guidance only and does not provide recovery actions.
Do not invent corrective actions, commands, or control changes not described in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to observe system startup or shutdown behavior
Access to system fault indication if available

access_required: Access to observe the system during startup or shutdown
Access to view system fault indication if available
system_preconditions: The system is currently in startup or shutdown, or appears stuck during one of those transitions.

visual_references.description: Lifecycle slide showing AGV progression through startup, sorting, bag-out, and shutdown, useful for recognizing startup and shutdown state context.
Overall System RMS page reference showing where system faults and active AGV faults would appear.

steps.title: Confirm the system is in startup or shutdown and assess whether the transition is delayed
steps.purpose: Establish that the issue being observed matches the source context of startup or shutdown transition visibility.
steps.instruction: Observe the system and determine whether it is currently in startup or shutdown. Check whether the transition appears delayed, incomplete, or not progressing as expected.
steps.expected_result: The current transition state is identified as startup or shutdown, and the observer has confirmed that the transition appears delayed or incomplete.
steps.healthy_condition: The system is clearly progressing through the expected startup or shutdown state behavior.
steps.failure_condition: The system appears stuck, delayed, or incomplete during startup or shutdown.

actions.label: Identify transition state
actions.exact_action: Observe whether the system is in startup or shutdown and whether the transition appears delayed or incomplete.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look at the lifecycle/state progression showing AGVs starting in shutdown, moving through startup, and returning to shutdown.

stop_or_escalate_if: The transition remains incomplete and the current state cannot be confidently identified from visible information.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: AGVs start up in a shutdown state at specific charging locations; startup takes them from power cycling to staged for sort; shutdown cycles them back to the charger.

steps.title: Check for a visible system fault indication
steps.purpose: Use the source-stated guidance that the system will indicate transition issues with system faults.
steps.instruction: Check whether the system is showing a system fault indication associated with the delayed startup or shutdown transition.
steps.expected_result: A visible system fault is either identified or ruled out based on what is available to observe.
steps.healthy_condition: No visible system fault is present, or the visible state is consistent with normal transition behavior.
steps.failure_condition: A visible system fault is present during the delayed or incomplete transition.

actions.label: Inspect fault indication
actions.exact_action: Check for a system fault indication because the source states that the system will tell you with system faults when transition issues occur.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for where system faults appear on the left and active AGV faults appear on the right of the Overall System RMS page.

stop_or_escalate_if: A visible fault is present but the source does not provide corrective action.
Fault visibility is expected but the required screen or access is unavailable.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The system will tell you with system faults; examples include an AGV waiting to do an exchange during shutdown or startup delay while waiting for an AGV on the charger.
Overall System RMS page shows system faults on the left and active AGV faults on the right.

steps.title: During shutdown, look for an AGV waiting to do an exchange
steps.purpose: Check the specific shutdown example given by the source for a visible waiting condition.
steps.instruction: If the system is in shutdown, look for the example condition described in the source: an AGV waiting to do an exchange.
steps.expected_result: The observer determines whether the shutdown delay matches the source example of an AGV waiting to do an exchange.
steps.healthy_condition: No AGV waiting-to-exchange condition is visible, or shutdown continues normally.
steps.failure_condition: An AGV waiting to do an exchange is visible during shutdown and appears to be associated with the delay.

actions.label: Inspect shutdown waiting condition
actions.exact_action: During shutdown, look for the example condition described in the source: an AGV waiting to do an exchange.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the lifecycle context for shutdown behavior while checking for the visible AGV waiting condition mentioned in the training segment.

stop_or_escalate_if: Shutdown remains incomplete and the visible condition cannot be matched to the source example.
A waiting-to-exchange condition is observed but the source provides no recovery action.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The system will tell you with system faults; examples include an AGV waiting to do an exchange during shutdown or startup delay while waiting for an AGV on the charger.
Shutdown may show an AGV waiting to do an exchange.

steps.title: During startup, look for delay while waiting for an AGV on the charger
steps.purpose: Check the specific startup example given by the source for a visible waiting condition.
steps.instruction: If the system is in startup, look for the example condition described in the source: a delay while waiting for an AGV on the charger.
steps.expected_result: The observer determines whether the startup delay matches the source example of waiting for an AGV on the charger.
steps.healthy_condition: No charger-related AGV waiting condition is visible, or startup continues normally.
steps.failure_condition: Startup appears delayed while waiting for an AGV on the charger.

actions.label: Inspect startup waiting condition
actions.exact_action: During startup, look for the example condition described in the source: a delay while waiting for an AGV on the charger.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the startup/shutdown lifecycle context while checking for a startup delay associated with an AGV on the charger.

stop_or_escalate_if: Startup remains incomplete and the visible condition cannot be matched to the source example.
A charger-related waiting condition is observed but the source provides no recovery action.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The system will tell you with system faults; examples include an AGV waiting to do an exchange during shutdown or startup delay while waiting for an AGV on the charger.
Startup may be delayed while waiting for an AGV on the charger.

steps.title: Record the observed fault or waiting condition
steps.purpose: Capture the visible condition exactly as observed so it can be compared to the source examples and escalated if needed.
steps.instruction: Record the visible waiting condition or fault indication exactly as observed and compare it to the source-described examples.
steps.expected_result: A clear observation record exists describing whether a visible system fault, shutdown exchange wait, or startup charger wait was observed.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed condition is documented clearly enough to support follow-up diagnosis.
steps.failure_condition: The transition remains incomplete and no source-recognizable fault or waiting condition can be identified.

actions.label: Document observation
actions.exact_action: Record the visible waiting condition or fault indication exactly as observed and compare it to the source-described examples.

stop_or_escalate_if: The transition remains incomplete and no source-recognizable fault or waiting condition can be identified.
The observed fault requires corrective action not described in this source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The system will tell you with system faults; examples include an AGV waiting to do an exchange during shutdown or startup delay while waiting for an AGV on the charger.

success_criteria: The observer identifies whether the delayed or incomplete startup or shutdown transition has a visible system fault indication.
The observer identifies whether the visible condition matches one of the source-described examples: AGV waiting to do an exchange during shutdown or waiting for an AGV on the charger during startup.
The observation is documented for follow-up or escalation.
healthy_conditions: No visible system fault is present during the transition.
No visible AGV waiting condition matching the source examples is present.
failure_conditions: Startup or shutdown remains delayed or incomplete.
A visible system fault is present during the transition.
During shutdown, an AGV is visibly waiting to do an exchange.
During startup, the system appears to be waiting for an AGV on the charger.
No source-recognizable fault or waiting condition can be identified even though the transition remains incomplete.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the transition remains incomplete and no source-recognizable fault or waiting condition can be identified.
Escalate if the observed fault requires corrective action not described in this source.
Escalate because this source does not provide recovery steps for clearing the fault.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Lifecycle and AGV state progression through startup and shutdown.
Overall System RMS fault areas showing system faults on the left and active AGV faults on the right.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The system will tell you with system faults; examples include an AGV waiting to do an exchange during shutdown or startup delay while waiting for an AGV on the charger.

missing_details: The source does not provide recovery or fault-clearing steps.
The source does not define exact screens, navigation steps, or operator inputs for checking the fault indication within this segment.
The source does not provide timing thresholds for when a startup or shutdown delay should be considered abnormal.
The source does not specify escalation destination or ownership beyond indicating escalation is needed when no recognizable condition is found or corrective action is not described.
